title: Monetary Policy Report—January 2025
summary: The Bank of Canada reduced its policy rate by 25 basis points to 3% and announced the end of quantitative tightening, with the overnight rate target at 3%, Bank Rate at 3.25%, and deposit rate at 2.95%. Inflation has been around 2% since August 2024 and is projected to remain near target over the forecast horizon, while Canadian economic growth is forecast to average 1.8% in 2025 and 2026. The threat of new US tariffs represents a major source of uncertainty, with the report noting that the outlook does not incorporate new tariffs but recognizes the threat is already affecting financial markets and business decisions.
key_points: Bank of Canada cut policy rate by 25 bps to 3%; Bank Rate at 3.25%, deposit rate at 2.95%
Inflation around 2% since August 2024; inflation expectations have largely normalized
Canadian GDP growth forecast at 1.8% for both 2025 and 2026
Global economy anticipated to grow at around 3%; US GDP growth projected to remain strong in 2025 then moderate in 2026
US tariff threat identified as major source of uncertainty affecting confidence, investment intentions, and contributing to Canadian dollar depreciation
why_it_matters: This quarterly monetary policy report from the Bank of Canada provides the official outlook on inflation, growth, and risks for the Canadian economy. The 25 bps rate cut signals ongoing easing bias while noting that inflation has normalized. The explicit identification of US tariff threats as a major uncertainty affecting financial markets and business decisions is significant for traders and analysts monitoring Canada-US trade dynamics. The illustrative scenario analysis on tariff impacts provides policymakers' quantitative assessment of potential downside risk.
macro_context: The report indicates the Canadian economy remains in modest excess supply with a soft labour market and some signs of slowing wage growth. Past interest rate cuts are contributing to increased household spending and housing activity. The economy is expected to gradually absorb excess supply over the projection horizon. Both upside and downside risks to the inflation outlook are roughly balanced absent new tariffs.
market_structure_context: The Bank of Canada announced the end of quantitative tightening alongside the rate cut, marking a shift in its balance sheet operations.

issues_found: Source is 14 months old (published 2025-01-29), exceeding 90-day recency threshold, but qualifies for acceptance as a historical policy document with clear archival value
Vote tally for rate decision not present in excerpted content (minor - not critical for understanding policy direction)
Illustrative tariff scenario referenced but not fully extracted from body text (expected for web excerpt)

human_review.notes: Historical record from January 2025. Source is a high-trust domain (Bank of Canada official publication). Rate decision and QT end noted. Tariff uncertainty flagged as key risk. Consider noting the age when using for current market analysis.

notes: Relevant to macro catalysts. Bank of Canada quarterly monetary policy report from January 2025.
